title: podman - copying host rootfs to the container
body:
### Issue Description

podman seems to copy the entire rootfs of the host.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Build by podman as `podman build --network host -f Dockerfile -t test/test .`
2. Run by podman as `podman run -ti --cap-add NET_RAW  --cap-add NET_ADMIN --device /dev/net/tun --restart unless-stopped test/test`


### Describe the results you received

When I login in the container, and I run `ls /etc`, I see it takes my same files, for example it replicated my `/etc/hosts` file.

### Describe the results you expected

Not replicated rootfs of the host.

comments.body:
Hi @D3vil0p3r , I am trying to reproduce this with `podman 4.5.0-dev` and I was unable to reproduce this.I tried this with following `Dockerfile`

```Dockerfile
FROM alpine
RUN echo hello
```

```console
[fl@fedora bin]$ ./podman build --network host -f Dockerfile -t test/test .
STEP 1/2: FROM alpine
Resolved "alpine" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 63b65145d645 done  
Copying config b2aa39c304 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2/2: RUN echo hello
hello
COMMIT test/test
--> ae2fbb84737
Successfully tagged localhost/test/test:latest
ae2fbb8473762c73a44b334f9da3d1c6d454b5990a64c95097dcd797d9a90cc3
[fl@fedora bin]$ ./podman run -ti --cap-add NET_RAW  --cap-add NET_ADMIN --device /dev/net/tun --restart unless-stopped test/test
/ # ls -a
.      ..     bin    dev    etc    home   lib    media  mnt    opt    proc   root   run    sbin   srv    sys    tmp    usr    var
/ # ls /etc
alpine-release  fstab           init.d          modprobe.d      mtab            os-release      profile.d       securetty       ssl             udhcpd.conf
apk             group           inittab         modules         network         passwd          protocols       services        ssl1.1
conf.d          hostname        issue           modules-load.d  nsswitch.conf   periodic        resolv.conf     shadow          sysctl.conf
crontabs        hosts           logrotate.d     motd            opt             profile         secfixes.d      shells          sysctl.d
/ # cat /etc/host
cat: can't open '/etc/host': No such file or directory
/ # ls -a
.      ..     bin    dev    etc    home   lib    media  mnt    opt    proc   root   run    sbin   srv    sys    tmp    usr    var
/ # cat /etc/host
hostname  hosts
/ # cat /etc/host
hostname  hosts
/ # cat /etc/hosts
127.0.0.1	localhost localhost.localdomain localhost4 localhost4.localdomain4
::1	localhost localhost.localdomain localhost6 localhost6.localdomain6
192.168.1.16	host.containers.internal
10.0.2.100	635d0007d30f naughty_einstein
/ # exit
```

Here the `/etc` was not same as my host instead it was rootfs of alpine.

@flouthoc thank you for your answer. Try to edit your `/etc/hosts` file by adding some new line with a random value.
I'm using the following Dockerfile:
```dockerfile
FROM athenaos/base:latest

ENV LANG=en_US.UTF-8
ENV TZ=Europe/Zurich
ENV PUSER=athena
ENV PUID=1000
ENV MOTD=/etc/motd

# Configure the locale; enable only en_US.UTF-8 and the current locale.
RUN sed -i -e 's~^\([^#]\)~#\1~' '/etc/locale.gen' && \
  echo -e '\nen_US.UTF-8 UTF-8' >> '/etc/locale.gen' && \
  if [[ "${LANG}" != 'en_US.UTF-8' ]]; then \
  echo "${LANG}" >> '/etc/locale.gen'; \
  fi && \
  locale-gen && \
  echo -e "LANG=${LANG}\nLC_ADDRESS=${LANG}\nLC_IDENTIFICATION=${LANG}\nLC_MEASUREMENT=${LANG}\nLC_MONETARY=${LANG}\nLC_NAME=${LANG}\nLC_NUMERIC=${LANG}\nLC_PAPER=${LANG}\nLC_TELEPHONE=${LANG}\nLC_TIME=${LANG}" > '/etc/locale.conf'

# Configure the timezone.
RUN echo "${TZ}" > /etc/timezone && \
  ln -sf "/usr/share/zoneinfo/${TZ}" /etc/localtime

RUN pacman -Syu --noconfirm

#######################################################
###                  BASIC PACKAGES                 ###
#######################################################

RUN pacman -Syu --noconfirm --needed accountsservice btrfs-progs dialog gcc inetutils make man-db man-pages most nano nbd net-tools netctl pv rsync sudo timelineproject-hg xdg-user-dirs

#######################################################
###                   DEPENDENCIES                  ###
#######################################################

RUN pacman -Syu --noconfirm --needed exa python-libtmux python-libtmux sassc hwloc ocl-icd pocl

#######################################################
###                      FONTS                      ###
#######################################################

RUN pacman -Syu --noconfirm --needed adobe-source-han-sans-cn-fonts adobe-source-han-sans-jp-fonts adobe-source-han-sans-kr-fonts gnu-free-fonts nerd-fonts-jetbrains-mono ttf-jetbrains-mono

#######################################################
###                    UTILITIES                    ###
#######################################################

RUN pacman -Syu --noconfirm --needed asciinema bashtop bat bc cmatrix cowsay cron downgrade dunst eog espeakup figlet file-roller fortune-mod git gnome-keyring imagemagick jdk-openjdk jq lolcat lsd neofetch nyancat openbsd-netcat openvpn orca p7zip paru pfetch polkit python-pywhat reflector sl textart tidy tk tmux toilet tree ufw unzip vim vnstat wget which xclip xcp xmlstarlet zoxide
RUN pacman -Syu --noconfirm --needed openssl shellinabox

#######################################################
###                ATHENA REPOSITORY                ###
#######################################################

RUN pacman -Syu --noconfirm --needed athena-application-config athena-nvchad athena-welcome athena-zsh figlet-fonts htb-tools myman nist-feed superbfetch-git toilet-fonts

# Copy the configuration files and scripts.
COPY rootfs/ /

RUN echo "cat $MOTD" >> /etc/zsh/zprofile
RUN systemd-machine-id-setup
RUN rm -rf /etc/skel/.bashrc.pacnew /etc/skel/.flag-work-once

RUN useradd -ms /bin/zsh $PUSER
RUN usermod -aG users,lp,network,power,sys,wheel -u "$PUID" $PUSER && echo "$PUSER ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD: ALL" > /etc/sudoers.d/$PUSER
RUN chmod 044 /etc/sudoers.d/$PUSER
RUN echo -e "$PUSER\n$PUSER" | passwd "$PUSER"
RUN sed -i "s/source ~\/.bash_aliases/source ~\/.bash_aliases\nsource ~\/.bashrc/g" /home/$PUSER/.zshrc

USER $PUSER:$PUSER
WORKDIR /home/$PUSER
RUN xdg-user-dirs-update

# Running as login shell for executing /etc/zsh/zprofile script and the custom MOTD
CMD ["/bin/zsh", "-l"]
```
I noted the issue is there also if you have your custom `rootfs` folder. It is not related to the build process because if I build by docker or podman, and I use `podman run`, I have the issue. I don't have this issue if I use `docker run`.

You can retrieve the container directly from Docker Hub by:
```
podman run -ti \
  --cap-add NET_RAW \
  --cap-add NET_ADMIN \
  --device /dev/net/tun \
  --restart unless-stopped \
  docker.io/athenaos/core:latest
```
The most strange fact is that by `docker run` I don't have this issue.

Container project source: https://github.com/Athena-OS/athena-core-docker

@D3vil0p3r If you only concerned with `/etc/hosts` files I'd suggest this: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/source/markdown/podman-run.1.md.in#L25 , you can always choose `--no-hosts` which allows users to use image's `/etc/hosts`.

> @D3vil0p3r If you only concerned with `/etc/hosts` files I'd suggest this: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/source/markdown/podman-run.1.md.in#L25 , you can always choose `--no-hosts` which allows users to use image's `/etc/hosts`.

It means that podman will replicate specific files if I don't exclude them by some arguments like `--no-hosts`? If so, this kind of files are only `/etc/hosts`, `/etc/hostname`, and `/etc/resolv.conf` or there are also other files that could be replicated in the container?
@D3vil0p3r Nope only these files are by default shared with the host.

> @D3vil0p3r Nope only these files are by default shared with the host.

Ok, perfect. Thank you for your support. I close the issue.
